questionText: Você encontra um amigo e quer saber como ele está passando. O que você diz?
correctAnswer: How are you getting on these days?
options: How are you getting on these days?
What are you doing?
Where have you been?
When will you arrive?

questionText: Alguém recomenda um filme para você. Como você responde de forma positiva e interessada?
correctAnswer: That sounds really good. I'll definitely check it out.
options: That sounds really good. I'll definitely check it out.
I don't want to watch it.
That's boring to me.
I'm too tired for movies.

questionText: Seu colega pergunta sua opinião sobre um show que vocês assistiram. Como você expressa uma opinião neutra e respeitosa?
correctAnswer: I thought it was interesting, though it wasn't really my style.
options: I thought it was interesting, though it wasn't really my style.
It was absolutely terrible.
I hated every minute of it.
You have bad taste in music.

questionText: Você quer convidar um amigo para ir ao cinema. Como você formula o convite?
correctAnswer: Would you like to come to the cinema with me this weekend?
options: Would you like to come to the cinema with me this weekend?
You must go to the cinema.
I'm forcing you to come.
Cinema is the best thing.

questionText: Alguém conta um segredo constrangedor sobre si mesmo. Como você responde com apoio?
correctAnswer: Don't worry, I understand. That happens to everyone.
options: Don't worry, I understand. That happens to everyone.
That's so embarrassing!
You're so weird.
I'll tell everyone about it.

questionText: Você quer saber mais detalhes sobre um livro que seu amigo está lendo. Como você pergunta?
correctAnswer: What's the book about? Is it good so far?
options: What's the book about? Is it good so far?
You don't read well.
Books are stupid.
I don't care about your book.

questionText: Você quer sugerir uma atividade diferente para fazer com amigos no fim de semana. Como você faz isso?
correctAnswer: Why don't we try something different this time, like going to a concert?
options: Why don't we try something different this time, like going to a concert?
We always do boring things.
You choose what we do.
I'm tired of you all.

questionText: Um amigo oferece para compartilhar seus ingressos para um evento. Como você agradece?
correctAnswer: That's really kind of you. Thank you so much!
options: That's really kind of you. Thank you so much!
I don't want your tickets.
You're being too generous.
Tickets are too expensive anyway.

questionText: Você está em um museu e quer chamar a atenção de alguém para um quadro interessante. Como você faz isso?
correctAnswer: Look at that painting! Don't you think it's amazing?
options: Look at that painting! Don't you think it's amazing?
That painting is very bad.
You should leave this museum.
Art is not important.

questionText: Seu amigo convida você para uma festa, mas você tem outra atividade marcada. Como você recusa educadamente?
correctAnswer: I'd love to come, but I've already made plans that evening.
options: I'd love to come, but I've already made plans that evening.
Your party is boring.
I hate all your friends.
You shouldn't have a party.

questionText: Você quer expressar que não está familiarizado com um gênero de música. Como você diz isso?
correctAnswer: I'm not really into that kind of music, but I'd be happy to listen.
options: I'm not really into that kind of music, but I'd be happy to listen.
That music is terrible.
You have no taste.
Music is useless.

questionText: Alguém pergunta sua opinião sobre uma série popular. Como você mostra entusiasmo moderado?
correctAnswer: I quite enjoyed it, though some episodes were better than others.
options: I quite enjoyed it, though some episodes were better than others.
It was the worst show ever.
Everyone who likes it is stupid.
I can't understand why you watch it.

questionText: Você quer combinar com um amigo a hora de encontro para um evento. Como você propõe isso?
correctAnswer: Should we meet at six o'clock outside the theater?
options: Should we meet at six o'clock outside the theater?
You always choose the time.
Meeting is unnecessary.
I'll just go alone.

questionText: Você descobre que perdeu um episódio importante de sua série favorita. Como você expressa decepção?
correctAnswer: Oh no! I missed an episode. Could someone tell me what happened?
options: Oh no! I missed an episode. Could someone tell me what happened?
I don't care about that series.
The series is not important.
Nobody watches it anyway.

questionText: Um amigo pede para você descrever um filme que você assistiu recentemente. Como você começa?
correctAnswer: It's quite an interesting film about a detective solving a mystery.
options: It's quite an interesting film about a detective solving a mystery.
I don't remember the title.
Films are all the same.
You wouldn't like it anyway.

questionText: Você quer expressar que uma performance teatral foi decepcionante. Como você comunica isso diplomaticamente?
correctAnswer: I appreciated the effort, but it didn't quite meet my expectations.
options: I appreciated the effort, but it didn't quite meet my expectations.
That was terrible acting.
The worst show I've ever seen.
Everyone performed badly.

questionText: Você quer saber se seu amigo já viu um filme famoso. Como você pergunta?
correctAnswer: Have you ever seen that famous film that everyone's talking about?
options: Have you ever seen that famous film that everyone's talking about?
You haven't watched enough films.
Films are not important.
I don't want to know your opinion.

questionText: Um colega sugere um plano de fim de semana que não o interessa muito. Como você recusa de forma amigável?
correctAnswer: Thanks for the invitation, but I'm not really in the mood for that right now.
options: Thanks for the invitation, but I'm not really in the mood for that right now.
That's a stupid idea.
You're wasting my time.
I don't want to be around you.

questionText: Você quer expressar que uma ator ou atriz tem um talento especial. Como você comenta isso?
correctAnswer: That actress really has a gift for bringing characters to life.
options: That actress really has a gift for bringing characters to life.
She can't act well.
I don't like her performance.
She should stop acting.

questionText: Alguém faz uma recomendação de música que você não conhece. Como você expressa curiosidade?
correctAnswer: I've never heard of that band. Tell me more about their style of music.
options: I've never heard of that band. Tell me more about their style of music.
That music is probably bad.
I don't want to learn anything new.
You have strange taste in music.

questionText: Você quer sugerir que assistir a um documentário seria educativo. Como você faz essa proposta?
correctAnswer: I think we could learn a lot from watching this documentary together.
options: I think we could learn a lot from watching this documentary together.
Documentaries are boring.
You need to educate yourself.
No one watches documentaries anymore.

questionText: Um amigo pergunta se você vai a um evento importante da cultura local. Como você responde mostrando interesse?
correctAnswer: Yes, I'm really looking forward to it. Have you been before?
options: Yes, I'm really looking forward to it. Have you been before?
I'm not interested in local events.
That event is not important.
I don't have time for culture.

questionText: Você quer expressar que prefere um tipo de entretenimento ao outro. Como você compara?
correctAnswer: I'd rather go to a concert than watch a film, but both are fun.
options: I'd rather go to a concert than watch a film, but both are fun.
Films are better than concerts.
Concerts are always worse.
You don't know what's good entertainment.

questionText: Alguém convida você para um festival de artes. Como você expressa que é uma oportunidade especial?
correctAnswer: That sounds like a wonderful opportunity. I've always wanted to experience something like that.
options: That sounds like a wonderful opportunity. I've always wanted to experience something like that.
Art festivals are a waste of time.
I'd rather stay home.
That's not worth my time.

questionText: Você encontra alguém que ama o mesmo tipo de livro que você. Como você demonstra essa conexão?
correctAnswer: Oh, you love that author too? That's great! Have you read their latest book?
options: Oh, you love that author too? That's great! Have you read their latest book?
You have bad taste in books.
That author is not popular.
Reading is not interesting.

questionText: Um amigo pergunta sua opinião sobre uma performance ao vivo que vocês assistiram juntos. Como você inicia a discussão?
correctAnswer: What did you think of the performance? Which part did you enjoy the most?
options: What did you think of the performance? Which part did you enjoy the most?
That performance was clearly bad.
You have no appreciation for art.
Nobody enjoyed that show.

questionText: Você quer pedir a um amigo que evite spoilers de um filme que você vai assistir. Como você expressa isso?
correctAnswer: Please don't tell me what happens in the film. I want to be surprised.
options: Please don't tell me what happens in the film. I want to be surprised.
You're stupid for spoiling films.
I don't care about spoilers.
Tell me everything about the film.

questionText: Você quer fazer um elogio autêntico sobre a atuação de um ator em um filme. Como você faz?
correctAnswer: The main actor was absolutely brilliant in that role.
options: The main actor was absolutely brilliant in that role.
That actor was boring.
I couldn't watch that performance.
The acting was terrible throughout.

questionText: Um amigo pergunta se você quer ir a um show de um artista que está tendo uma fase de pausa na carreira. Como você responde com diplomacia?
correctAnswer: I'd love to see them perform again. It's always exciting when an artist makes a comeback.
options: I'd love to see them perform again. It's always exciting when an artist makes a comeback.
That artist is not famous anymore.
Why would we go to see them?
Their music is outdated now.

questionText: Você quer debater sobre diferenças culturais no entretenimento entre dois países. Como você introduz o tópico?
correctAnswer: I've noticed that entertainment preferences vary quite a lot between cultures. Don't you think?
options: I've noticed that entertainment preferences vary quite a lot between cultures. Don't you think?
Your culture has bad taste.
Entertainment is the same everywhere.
I don't want to discuss this.

questionText: Alguém critica um filme que você ama. Como você defende sua opinião sem ser agressivo?
correctAnswer: I understand it might not be everyone's cup of tea, but I really connected with it.
options: I understand it might not be everyone's cup of tea, but I really connected with it.
You're wrong about this film.
Only stupid people don't like this film.
Your opinion doesn't matter.

questionText: Você quer expressar que uma adaptação de livro para filme não fez justiça à história original. Como você comenta isso?
correctAnswer: The book was so much richer in detail. The film adaptation didn't quite capture that depth.
options: The book was so much richer in detail. The film adaptation didn't quite capture that depth.
The film was better than the book.
Books are always worse than films.
That adaptation was perfect.

questionText: Um amigo sugere que vocês explorem um gênero de arte que você nunca experimentou antes. Como você responde de forma aberta?
correctAnswer: I'm willing to give it a try. It could be an interesting experience for me.
options: I'm willing to give it a try. It could be an interesting experience for me.
That's a waste of my time.
I only like what I already know.
New things are always bad.

questionText: Você quer expressar que uma série de TV mudou sua perspectiva sobre um assunto importante. Como você comunica isso?
correctAnswer: That series really opened my eyes to a different perspective on the issue.
options: That series really opened my eyes to a different perspective on the issue.
I don't watch series to learn.
TV shows are not educational.
Nothing can change my mind.

questionText: Um colega pergunta sua opinião sobre um artista contemporâneo que divide opiniões. Como você responde de forma equilibrada?
correctAnswer: Their work is certainly thought-provoking, though I can see why it's controversial.
options: Their work is certainly thought-provoking, though I can see why it's controversial.
That artist is terrible.
Everyone agrees that artist is bad.
Art should be simple and obvious.

questionText: Você quer sugerir que explorar a cultura de outro país através da arte e cinema seria valioso. Como você propõe isso?
correctAnswer: Watching films from different countries could help us understand their culture better.
options: Watching films from different countries could help us understand their culture better.
Other cultures are not interesting.
We should only watch films from our country.
Culture is not important.

questionText: Alguém propõe um projeto criativo que envolve colaboração com artistas locais. Como você expressa seu apoio?
correctAnswer: That's a fantastic idea. Supporting local artists is really important for the community.
options: That's a fantastic idea. Supporting local artists is really important for the community.
Local artists are not worth our time.
We should only support famous people.
Community projects are a waste.

questionText: Você quer expressar que um documentário sobre um assunto histórico forneceu contexto importante. Como você comenta?
correctAnswer: The documentary provided excellent background on the historical events we were discussing.
options: The documentary provided excellent background on the historical events we were discussing.
Documentaries are boring.
History is not relevant.
That documentary was a waste of time.

questionText: Um amigo questiona por que você gasta tempo com um hobbie artístico específico. Como você justifica sua paixão?
correctAnswer: It brings me joy and allows me to express myself creatively, which is important to me.
options: It brings me joy and allows me to express myself creatively, which is important to me.
You wouldn't understand why I do it.
Art is pointless.
I'm just wasting my time.

questionText: Você quer sugerir que as tendências de entretenimento mudam rapidamente e é importante se manter atualizado. Como você expressa isso?
correctAnswer: Entertainment trends change so quickly these days. It's hard to keep up with everything.
options: Entertainment trends change so quickly these days. It's hard to keep up with everything.
Entertainment never changes.
We should ignore new trends.
Keeping up is not necessary.

questionText: Alguém pergunta por que a representatividade na mídia é importante. Como você explica seu ponto de vista?
correctAnswer: Because people need to see themselves represented in films and shows to feel valued and understood.
options: Because people need to see themselves represented in films and shows to feel valued and understood.
Representation doesn't matter in entertainment.
Everyone watches the same things anyway.
It's not the responsibility of media.

questionText: Você quer expressar que a streaming mudou fundamentalmente como consumimos entretenimento. Como você comenta isso?
correctAnswer: Streaming services have really transformed how we access and consume entertainment nowadays.
options: Streaming services have really transformed how we access and consume entertainment nowadays.
Streaming hasn't changed anything.
People watch less entertainment now.
Traditional media is still the same.

questionText: Um colega sugere que assistir a artes cênicas ao vivo é uma experiência diferente de ver filmes. Como você concorda e expande o ponto?
correctAnswer: Absolutely. There's something unique about the live energy and spontaneity of theater.
options: Absolutely. There's something unique about the live energy and spontaneity of theater.
Theater is outdated compared to film.
Live performances are less professional.
Films and theater are exactly the same.

questionText: Você quer sugerir que a música tem o poder de unir pessoas de diferentes origens. Como você exprime isso?
correctAnswer: Music has this incredible ability to bring people together across cultural boundaries.
options: Music has this incredible ability to bring people together across cultural boundaries.
Music only divides people.
Everyone has the same musical taste.
Cultural differences make no difference in music.
